claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you enquiring about for Child Benefit, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're enquiring about Child Benefit for three children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some important details. Let me start with the first question:

**What are the dates of birth for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8 years old, and Charlie is 2 years old.

Now I need to understand your living situation. Here's my next question:

**Do all three children currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know. Now I need to check an important detail about your residency status:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you have a right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now let me gather some important information about your employment and income situation:

**Are you currently working, and if so, are you receiving any income from employment or self-employment?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I can answer questions about my children and our living situation, but is that relevant for Child Benefit eligibility? I thought Child Benefit was available regardless of whether I work or not?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're absolutely right, and I apologize for that question! Child Benefit eligibility isn't based on whether you work or your income level - thank you for pointing that out.

Let me refocus on the relevant information. I need to check a few more important details about your circumstances:

**Are you currently receiving any benefits such as Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, or income-related Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure why you're asking about my benefits - I just want to know if I can claim Child Benefit for my three children who live with me. Is there something specific about their situation you need to know?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're absolutely right, and I apologize for overcomplicating this. Let me refocus on what's actually relevant for Child Benefit eligibility.

I have the key information I need:
- You have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (2 years old)
- All three children live with you in Manchester
- You're a UK resident

Before I can make my final assessment, I just need to check one important thing:

**Are any of your children currently in hospital, in care, or living away from home for any reason?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're all at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the Child Benefit eligibility rules to provide you with a proper answer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: **Alex, Blake, and Charlie**.

**Summary:**
- You meet the eligibility requirements because you live in the UK and are responsible for all three children who live with you
- All three children are under 16 years old
- None of them are in hospital or care
- Therefore, all three children qualify for Child Benefit

You can now proceed to make a claim with HMRC. If you have any other questions about your claim, HMRC's Child Benefit Office will be able to help you further.

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex and they live together. Alex meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, lives with the responsible person in the UK, and is not in hospital or care. Alex is eligible for Child Benefit.

Blake is 8 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Blake and they live together. Blake meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, lives with the responsible person in the UK, and is not in hospital or care. Blake is eligible for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Charlie and they live together. Charlie meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, lives with the responsible person in the UK, and is not in hospital or care. Charlie is eligible for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident responsible for three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) who all live with them in Manchester. All three children are under 16 years old, live with the claimant, and are not in hospital or care. The claimant meets the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three children: they are responsible for each child, the children live with them, and they reside in the UK. All three children are therefore eligible for Child Benefit.
